---
title: "🔵 Forecasting"
subtitle: "Probabilistic forecasting, model evaluation, and where forecasting stops being causal inference."
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
page-layout: full
listing:
  - id: forecasting-posts
    contents:
      - "../posts/**/*.qmd"
    include:
      categories: ["forecasting"]
    sort: "date asc"
    type: default
    fields: [date, title, description, reading-time]
    max-items: 30
---

Forecasting answers a different question than causal inference — not
*what would have happened otherwise*, but *what is likely to happen
next*. Both matter. Neither replaces the other. The posts on this page
walk through the methods I use and, more importantly, the ones I
choose not to use.

If you're coming from the causal-inference arc, start with *Forecasting
Is Not Causal Inference* — it's the bridge between the two.

---

## Posts in this series

::: {#forecasting-posts}
:::

---
